name: Ruby Razz Crunch
recipeIngredient: 10 oz. thawed frozen red raspberries (reserve 1/4 cup)
1 lb. thawed frozen rhubarb (cut into pieces if necessary)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 325°F.
2. Bake the fruit mixture for 55 to 65 minutes, or until done.
3. Drain: Reserve the juice from the baked fruit and set aside.
4. Mix: In a saucepan, combine the reserved fruit juices with 1 cup of sugar (add water if needed).
5. Combine: In another saucepan, mix 1/2 cup sugar and 3 tbsp. cornstarch. Stir in the fruit juices. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly until thick and clear.
6. Remove from heat; cover.
7. Blend together: In a large bowl, sift 1 1/2 cups flour, 1 cup brown sugar, 1 cup quick-cooking rolled oats, 1 tsp. cinnamon, and 1/2 cup melted butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
8. Press two-thirds of the crumb mixture into a greased 9 x 9 pan. Spread the drained fruit mixture over the crumb layer and sprinkle with the remaining crumb mixture.
9. Bake at 325°F for 25 to 30 minutes, or until golden brown. Cool slightly in the pan before cutting into squares.
10. Serve warm or cold with mounds of topping.
Pink Frozen Cream Topping:
1. Beat 1 cup whipping cream until thickened; add 1/4 cup sugar, the reserved raspberries, and 1 to 3 drops of red food coloring. Continue beating until stiff. Drop onto waxed paper or aluminum foil and freeze until firm. (Serve unfrozen if desired.)
